authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary U.S. banking-agency guidance for third-party lifecycle, monitoring, issue escalation, and risk management that directly shapes sponsor-bank and embedded-finance dependency support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Dependency classification, third-party incident handling, and escalation ownership
used_for: evidence expectations for vendor and bank-partner issues

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint banking-agency fintech guide describing operational resilience, information security, incident response, audit, complaints, and contractual controls relevant to fintech platform support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Platform-prerequisite validation and dependency evidence review
used_for: tenant facts that distinguish fintech programs from generic support
operational due-diligence domains that become support dependencies

authoritative_sources.title: Computer-Security Incident Notification Requirements for Banking Organizations and Their Bank Service Providers
authoritative_sources.publisher: Federal Register / Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary banking-agency rule for notifiable incidents and bank service provider notification duties, which materially affects fintech outage triage and communication boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Incident severity assessment and regulated escalation timing
used_for: refusal to downplay or close potentially reportable incidents

authoritative_sources.title: Architecture, Infrastructure, and Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interagency IT handbook booklet covering operations governance, infrastructure and operations controls, resilience, and common operational risk topics for financial institutions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Service intake, operational dependency mapping, and operations-state normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Development, Acquisition, and Maintenance
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interagency IT handbook booklet covering secure development, API risk mitigation, testing, maintenance, documentation, and post-implementation review relevant to change-readiness support.

authoritative_sources.title: Retail Payment Systems
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized FFIEC workflow reference on payment instruments, clearing and settlement context, operational risk, and vendor or third-party payment dependencies that differentiate fintech platform support from generic support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment-rail dependency analysis and stakeholder-safe impact framing
used_for: ACH, card, and A2A dependency context

authoritative_sources.title: PCI DSS v4.0.1
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized payment-card control standard defining access control, logging, change management, and incident-response evidence expectations for card-enabled fintech environments.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access, logging, change, and incident evidence sufficiency review
used_for: change-readiness refusal triggers when evidence is incomplete

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and operating-context validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the product, payment rail, environment, partner model, affected capability, requester intent, and named systems of record before stating whether the request is a normal support case, a dependency issue, an access case, or a change-readiness review.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-of-truth retrieval across the fintech stack
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and compare incident, ledger, processor, gateway, webhook, IAM, change, and partner-support records to establish current workflow state and identify whether the platform truth set is complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported troubleshooting and service-state normalization
workflow_stages.description: Summarize incident, access, or dependency status using only authoritative platform records and payment-context terminology without implying unowned security, release, or financial decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Change-readiness and interface dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Review testing, rollback, approval, API, and integration evidence before a platform change can be described as ready, and block the work when required artifacts are missing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and stakeholder-safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Produce resolution notes, incident summaries, and customer or stakeholder communication only when owner, impact, and next-step posture are grounded in named records and approved communication boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority, repeat-failure, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the request crosses into security, engineering, release approval, fraud, compliance, or partner-bank authority, or when repeated failures or source conflicts make autonomous support unsafe.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: incident or service ticket with severity, timestamps, affected product, and impact statement
sponsor-bank, processor, vendor, or partner support notices and escalation references
core ledger, issuer processor, ACH, gateway, webhook, and observability records used to establish platform truth
IAM request, approval chain, role map, and segregation-of-duties evidence for access-related cases
change ticket, integrated test evidence, rollback runbook, approver record, and release window artifacts
customer or stakeholder communication summaries, complaint signals, and escalation packets
audit, logging, and control evidence relevant to access, change, and incident handling
systems_of_record: issuer processor, payment gateway, ACH processor, or payment-network support systems
API gateway, integration monitoring, and webhook delivery systems
change management and release tracking systems
observability, logging, and status page systems
CRM, complaint, or stakeholder communication systems

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on financial-platform-support ownership.
Request the minimum missing tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed for safe progression.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, incident summaries, communication summaries, and escalation packets tied to named fintech artifacts.
Resolve supported service requests when authoritative platform status can be established without taking security, engineering, compliance, or approval ownership.
escalate: Required product, partner, environment, approval, or record-of-truth context is missing or contradictory.
The request requires security-forensics, privileged-access approval, release approval, payment adjustment approval, fraud judgment, or legal or compliance interpretation.
Sponsor-bank, processor, vendor, or tenant policies materially conflict on incident communication, access handling, or change approval.
Repeat failures, notifiable incidents, or unresolved dependency issues change the risk posture beyond routine support.
refuse: Requests to bypass access controls, testing, rollback evidence, logging, or approval workflow just to restore service or speed deployment.
Requests to fabricate readiness, closure, or contained-status claims without authoritative records.
Requests to perform legal, compliance, fraud, engineering, or financial approval decisions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: treating a sponsor-bank, processor, or payment-rail outage like generic SaaS downtime without settlement, card-network, or notification implications
granting or implying access restoration without role, approval, or segregation-of-duties evidence
marking a fintech platform change ready without testing, rollback, or approval artifacts
declaring a dependency issue resolved when ledger, processor, gateway, or webhook records still conflict
using generic incident language and omitting fintech artifacts such as BIN sponsorship, ledger-of-record, processor ticket, settlement cutoff, or partner maintenance window
letting repeat failures close locally instead of escalating with recurrence history and owner accountability
allowing support work to drift into security forensics, engineering signoff, fraud or compliance adjudication, or financial approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Whether a specific fintech vendor or program manager is a bank service provider with notification duties depends on the tenant's legal structure, contract, and sponsor-bank relationship.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of partner role and contract context; escalate when incident-notification authority depends on those facts.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Access and release approval ownership varies across sponsor-bank, processor, and tenant operating models even for similar fintech products.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant approval matrices and named owners at runtime; refuse unsupported assumptions about approval authority.
